what_it_does: Defines local CSS for a Platform UI kit, including the app shell, sidebar navigation, top bar, buttons, search, media tiles, panels, form controls, chat pill, tabs/dividers, calendar views, and supporting layout utilities.
observations: Sets global box sizing, full-height root elements, body typography, canvas background, antialiasing, and hidden body overflow.
Uses a two-column shell with a fixed-width sidebar and scrollable main content area.
Defines sidebar branding, logo placeholder, menu headers, navigation items, collapsible-style section titles, and dashed content slots.
Styles a top-right notification/avatar bar with badge treatments.
Provides button variants for outline, primary, dashed, ghost, dark, and icon-only states.
Includes a baked-in search control with icon and input styling.
Defines media tile grids, selected tile checkmarks, and video play overlays.
Provides soft gold panels, field labels, text inputs, help text, checkbox states, and a dropzone.
Adds a fixed rounded chat pill with icon and unread count badge.
Defines calendar task cards, month navigation, calendar grid cells, day states, and colored event chips.
standouts: Broad component coverage is concentrated in one file.
Dashed borders, gold accents, and soft gold backgrounds recur across placeholders, actions, panels, and states.
Calendar styling covers both task summary rows and full month grid event chips.
conventions: Uses a consistent `cv-` class prefix with modifier classes such as `cv-btn--primary` and state classes such as `active`, `selected`, `off`, and `muted`.
Organizes sections with comment dividers by UI area.
Leans on CSS custom properties for typography, colors, radii, shadows, and easing.
Uses flexbox and CSS grid for most layout structures.
Uses pseudo-elements for divider lines, selected checkmarks, and checkbox ticks.
Mixes token-based colors with several hard-coded hex colors for badges, hover states, and calendar categories.
concerns: The file fixes the shell to two columns and does not include responsive breakpoints.
Several grids use fixed repeat counts, including five media columns and eight task columns, which may overflow in narrower containers.
Some color values are hard-coded instead of routed through custom properties.
Body overflow is hidden, so scrolling depends on `.cv-main` and fixed overlays may need careful containment.
notes: This file is the local CSS surface for a token-driven platform UI kit with sidebar navigation, action controls, forms, media tiles, chat, and calendar components.
questions: Are the hard-coded status colors intended to become design tokens?
Should this kit include responsive behavior for narrow screens?
Are the custom checkbox and navigation controls paired with accessible HTML states elsewhere?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it provides Platform UI kit local styles layered through design-token variables.
apparent_themes: Dashed placeholders and soft panel surfaces
Dense platform navigation and operational views
Component-state styling through modifiers and state classes
intention_signals: It appears intended to turn shared color/type tokens into a concrete platform application shell and reusable component set for ConceptV screens.
novelty: The repeated dashed placeholder language, soft gold panels, fixed chat pill, and detailed calendar chip system give the file a distinct platform-kit vocabulary.
contribution: Contributes a complete platform UI layer: shell layout, sidebar/navigation treatments, button and input variants, media tile states, soft panel/form patterns, chat affordance, and calendar/task visual structures that a fused system could preserve as component styling options.
summary_for_embedding: CSS file defining the ConceptV Platform UI kit layout and component styles for shell, sidebar, toolbar, buttons, search, media tiles, panels, forms, chat, and calendar views.
intention_for_embedding: This was for applying shared design tokens to a concrete platform interface kit with reusable navigation, action, form, media, communication, and scheduling UI patterns.
